sources.public_tuition_annual_usd: College Board Trends in College Pricing — published average published in-state tuition and required fees, public four-year institutions (nominal US$). Sparse annual anchors; intermediate years are linearly interpolated in the API.
sources.median_gross_rent_monthly_usd: U.S. Census Bureau historical median gross rent tables (nominal US$ per month). Sparse annual anchors; intermediate years are linearly interpolated in the API.
sources.refrigerator_usd: Representative retail price anchors for a standard refrigerator (nominal US$). Contextual reference prices for affordability hours, not a single SKU time series.
sources.washing_machine_usd: Representative retail price anchors for a standard clothes washer (nominal US$). Contextual reference prices for affordability hours, not a single SKU time series.
sources.television_usd: Representative retail price anchors for a color television (nominal US$). Contextual reference prices for affordability hours, not a single SKU time series.
sources.household_goods_methodology: Historical prices for some durable goods are estimated using official price indices anchored to benchmark price observations. Comparisons should be interpreted as approximate indicators of changing affordability.
sources.hours_of_work_methodology: Hours-of-work estimates use historical wage proxies to approximate how many hours an average worker would need to work to cover the selected expense. The series are intended as contextual affordability signals rather than precise household budget calculations.
sources.hours_of_work_wage: FRED AHETPI — BLS average hourly earnings of production and nonsupervisory employees, total private (nominal US$/hour, 1964+). Used for rent, tuition, and household-goods hours charts. FRED CES0500000003 (all private employees) begins in 2006 and is not used here so charts can extend further back; AHETPI excludes supervisory workers and may differ slightly in level from the all-employee series in overlapping years.
sources.hours_for_month_rent: U.S. Census Bureau median gross rent (reference anchors, interpolated) ÷ FRED AHETPI.
sources.hours_for_year_tuition: College Board published in-state public four-year tuition (reference anchors, interpolated) ÷ FRED AHETPI.
sources.household_goods_wage: FRED AHETPI — same wage series as other hours-of-work charts (see hours_of_work_wage).
sources.hours_for_refrigerator: Estimated refrigerator price (CPI household furnishings proxy, FRED CUSR0000SAH3, anchored to 2000 = US$825) ÷ FRED AHETPI.
sources.hours_for_washing_machine: Estimated washing-machine price (PPI household laundry equipment, FRED WPU12410220 through 2016 then WPU1241 major appliances, anchored to 2000 = US$650) ÷ FRED AHETPI.
sources.hours_for_television: Estimated television price (CPI video and audio, FRED CUSR0000SERA from 1994, anchored to 2000 = US$450; 1970–1993 from published retail price anchors with linear interpolation) ÷ FRED AHETPI.
sources.hours_for_vacuum_cleaner: Estimated vacuum-cleaner price (CPI household furnishings proxy, FRED CUSR0000SAH3, anchored to 2000 = US$120) ÷ FRED AHETPI.

hours_of_work.methodology_note: Hours-of-work estimates use historical wage proxies to approximate how many hours an average worker would need to work to cover the selected expense. The series are intended as contextual affordability signals rather than precise household budget calculations.
hours_of_work.wage_source: FRED AHETPI — BLS average hourly earnings of production and nonsupervisory employees, total private (1964+).
hours_of_work.wage_tradeoffs: FRED CES0500000003 (average hourly earnings of all private employees) begins in 2006. AHETPI extends to 1964 but covers production and nonsupervisory workers only; levels may differ from the all-employee series where both exist.

household_goods.methodology_note: Historical prices for some durable goods are estimated using official price indices anchored to benchmark price observations. Comparisons should be interpreted as approximate indicators of changing affordability.

refrigerator.label: Refrigerator
refrigerator.cpi_source: BLS CPI household furnishings and operations (FRED CUSR0000SAH3); used as proxy for major-appliance retail prices before item-level CPI history is available.

washing_machine.label: Washing machine
washing_machine.cpi_source: BLS PPI household laundry equipment (FRED WPU12410220, 1947–2016); continued with PPI major appliances (FRED WPU1241) from 2017.

television.label: Television
television.cpi_source: BLS CPI video and audio (FRED CUSR0000SERA, 1994+), anchored to benchmark retail price.

vacuum_cleaner.label: Vacuum cleaner
vacuum_cleaner.cpi_source: BLS CPI household furnishings and operations (FRED CUSR0000SAH3); used as proxy for small-appliance retail prices.
